function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

spellcheck.description: Whether to spellcheck the provided query.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.

function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information. Returns up to 5 results by default with titles, URLs, and descriptions. The query is passed through to the configured backend, so operators such as site:domain, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, and "exact phrase" may work when the backend supports them.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web. You may include backend-supported operators such as site:example.com, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, or "exact phrase".

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

messages.content:
Research the macro competitive landscape for Paraguay residency. People considering Paraguay usually also consider: Panama, Uruguay, Portugal D7, Spain NLV, Thailand, UAE, Costa Rica. For each:

1. What's the minimum investment or income required?
2. What's the tax rate on foreign income? 
3. How long to get residency?
4. Physical presence requirements?
5. Path to citizenship?
6. Cost of living comparison?
7. Key strengths and weaknesses vs Paraguay

Search for "Paraguay vs Panama residency", "Portugal D7 requirements 2026", "Spain non lucrative visa vs Paraguay", "Uruguay residency for Europeans", etc. Build a comparison table Nexa can use on their site and in marketing to handle the "why Paraguay not X" question their prospects ask.
I'll research each competitor systematically. Let me start with multiple parallel searches.

        "description": "Key Takeaway: Residency in Costa Rica offers four main pathways: Pensionado ($1,000/mo pension), Rentista ($2,500/mo passive income), Inversionista ($150,000 investment), and Digital Nomad ($3,000/mo remote work). All lead to permanent residency in 3 years, then citizenship in 7 years. Costa Rica’s territorial tax system exempts foreign-source income from local taxation, making it a powerful tool for non-US citizens. US citizens, however, still owe US federal tax on worldwide income regardless of residence.
[...]
The Pensionado category is built for retirees and those with passive pension income. The requirement is simple: you need a guaranteed, verifiable pension of at least $1,000 USD per month. That pension can come from a government retirement scheme, military service, Social Security, a private pension provider, or a lifetime annuity. [...]
The Rentista category targets people with passive income from investments, business earnings, or other sources. It can be investment returns, rental income, business distributions, or guaranteed payments.
[...]
The catch is you have to prove the income will continue for the next two years. Most applicants meet this requirement by making a bank deposit of approximately $60,000 at a Costa Rican bank, which then generates the required passive income through interest. The math is straightforward: $60,000 at 5% annual return equals roughly $250 per month, and most banks offer rates higher than that.         "description": "Living the Pura Vida lifestyle in Costa Rica comes with many perks, but it's important for expats to understand their tax obligations. Costa Rica's tax system is quite unique. It follows a territorial tax principle, meaning only income earned within Costa Rica is subject to taxation. Below we break down the key aspects of Costa Rican taxes for individuals and businesses, annual filing duties, and tips to stay compliant while enjoying paradise.
[...]
Costa Rica operates on a territorial tax system, which taxes only income from Costa Rican sources. In other words, foreign-sourced income is not taxed in Costa Rica. This principle is established by Costa Rican law: Article 1 of the Income Tax Law (Law No. 7092 - Ley del Impuesto sobre la Renta) defines Costa Rican-source income as income generated exclusively within Costa Rican territory (from services provided, goods located, capital invested, or rights used in Costa Rica). An exception was introduced for certain foreign passive incomes of multinational companies without local substance, to comply with international standards, but for most individual expats the rule is simple: foreign income is out of Costa Rica's tax scope.

[...]
Corporate Taxes: If you start a business or incorporate a company in Costa Rica, you'll encounter corporate income tax, known as Impuesto sobre las Utilidades. The standard corporate income tax rate is 30% of net profits. Net profit is calculated as gross income from Costa Rican activities minus deductible expenses. Costa Rica's territorial principle applies to companies too: a corporation is taxed on profits from activities in Costa Rica, while income earned abroad is outside the tax base.

Costa Rica's tax system is straightforward once you understand its territorial nature. For most expats, the main takeaway is simple: foreign income isn't taxed, but local earnings are.",

{"path": "/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/paraguay-competitive-landscape-analysis.md", "content": "# Macro Competitive Landscape: Paraguay Residency vs. Alternatives
## Prepared for Nexa Paraguay (nexa.paragu-ai.com)
### Research Date: May 2026

---

## 1. Executive Summary

Prospects comparing Paraguay to alternatives typically fall into three segments:
- **Tax optimizers** (comparing Panama, UAE, Costa Rica, Uruguay)
- **EU passport seekers** (comparing Portugal D7, Spain NLV)
- **Lifestyle expats** (comparing Thailand, Costa Rica, Uruguay)

**Paraguay's unique position**: The only jurisdiction combining ALL of: 0% foreign income tax, citizenship in 3 years (fastest in western hemisphere), zero physical presence requirement to maintain residency, no CRS reporting, and ultra-low cost of living — all with a $0 income threshold for the standard residency route.

---

## 2. Country-by-Country Analysis

### 2.1 PANAMA — Qualified Investor Visa (QIP)

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | $300K real estate (until Oct 15, 2026, then $500K). Alternate: $500K stocks or $750K bank deposit. Gov fees: $10K+ |
| **Tax on Foreign Income** | Territorial (0% on foreign-source income). But local income taxed up to 25% |
| **Time to Residency** | 30-90 days to permanent residency (day 1) |
| **Physical Presence** | Visit once every 2 years. No minimum days per visit |
| **Path to Citizenship** | 5 years of PR + Spanish exam + civics test. Total timeline: 6-8 years |
| **Cost of Living** | ~33% more expensive than Paraguay. Rent is 178% higher. Panama City 1BR: $915/mo vs Asuncion $374/mo |
| **CRS Reporting** | YES — Panama reports under CRS |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:
- Faster initial processing (30-90 days vs 4-9 months even under SUACE)
- Better banking infrastructure and dollarized economy
- Closer to US/Europe with major international hub (Tocumen)
- Special passport for Qualified Investors (2025 law)

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- $300K-$500K minimum investment vs $0+ for standard Paraguay route
- Citizenship takes 5+ years vs 3 years in Paraguay
- CRS reporting active (Paraguay has NO CRS)
- Higher cost of living (33%+ higher)
- Higher local income tax (up to 25% vs 10%)
- $300K threshold expires Oct 2026 — uncertainty

---

### 2.2 URUGUAY — Residency by Investment / Tax Holiday

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | Immigration: ~$180K real estate (common threshold, no fixed legal minimum). Tax holiday: $2M real estate OR $100K/yr innovation fund OR 183 days presence |
| **Tax on Foreign Income** | CRITICAL 2026 CHANGE: New residents after Jan 1, 2026 — foreign capital income taxed at 12% outside holiday. Holiday exempts foreign capital income for 11 years |
| **Time to Residency** | Temporary: 2-3 weeks. Permanent: 6-8 months. Total: ~1 year |
| **Physical Presence** | 60 days/year for residency maintenance. 183 days/year for tax residency via presence path |
| **Path to Citizenship** | 3 years of residency (with family) or 5 years. Spanish required. Dual citizenship allowed |
| **Cost of Living** | ~36% more expensive than Paraguay. Rent 50% higher. Montevideo 1BR: ~$750 vs Asuncion $374 |
| **CRS Reporting** | YES — Uruguay is an OECD CRS signatory |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:
- Stronger rule of law, investment-grade credit rating
- More European lifestyle, safer, better infrastructure
- Stable democracy, "Switzerland of South America"
- Better quality of life metrics (education, healthcare)

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- 2026 tax reform DESTROYED the previous advantage. Foreign income now 12% after 11-year holiday (vs 0% permanently in Paraguay)
- Tax holiday now requires $2M real estate or $100K/yr investment — astronomically higher than Paraguay
- CRS reporting active (Paraguay has none)
- Higher cost of living across all categories
- No direct citizenship by investment — must complete full residency period
- More complex bureaucracy and higher professional fees

---

### 2.3 PORTUGAL D7 — Passive Income Visa

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | €920/month passive income (€11,040/yr) for main applicant. Plus €11K in Portuguese bank account. No investment required |
| **Tax on Foreign Income** | CRITICAL: NHR regime ENDED Jan 2024. New IFICI regime only benefits researchers/tech. Standard progressive IRS up to 48%. Foreign pensions taxed at progressive rates. Foreign dividends/rental: 28% flat |
| **Time to Residency** | 6-12 months for visa + biometrics. Initial 2-year permit issued |
| **Physical Presence** | 183+ days/year (6 months minimum). This is strictly enforced |
| **Path to Citizenship** | 5 years of residency. Portuguese language test A2. EU passport granted (visa-free to 189 countries) |
| **Cost of Living** | ~50% higher than Paraguay. Rent 66% higher. Lisbon 1BR: €925 vs Asuncion €395. But outside Lisbon/Lagos, more affordable |
| **CRS Reporting** | YES — full EU tax transparency |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:
- EU citizenship pathway — the single most valuable passport in the world (189 visa-free countries)
- No lump-sum investment required (only income proof)
- European lifestyle, healthcare, and infrastructure
- Schengen zone access

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- NHR tax regime DEAD — foreign income now taxed up to 48% (Paraguay: 0% permanently)
- Mandatory physical presence 183+ days/year (Paraguay: once every 3 years)
- Slower path to citizenship: 5 years + A2 language test (Paraguay: 3 years + basic Spanish)
- Cost of living 50-55% higher
- CRS reporting to EU member states
- No territorial tax system — worldwide income taxed
- Wealth tax considerations in some EU countries

---

### 2.4 SPAIN NLV — Non-Lucrative Visa

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | €28,800/yr passive income/savings for main applicant (400% IPREM). +€7,200 per dependent. No investment required |
| **Tax on Foreign Income** | Worldwide income taxed at progressive rates 19-47%. Wealth tax 0.2-3.5% on global assets over €700K (unless in Madrid/Andalusia with relief). Modelo 720 foreign asset reporting |
| **Time to Residency** | 2 weeks - 3 months consular processing. Initial 1-year visa, then 2+2 renewals |
| **Physical Presence** | 183+ days/year mandatory. Strictly enforced at renewal |
| **Path to Citizenship** | 10 years of residency (5 years for Latin American nationals). Spanish exam DELE A2+ CCSE. Dual citizenship not allowed for most nationalities |
| **Cost of Living** | ~87% higher than Paraguay. Rent 133% higher. Madrid 1BR: €898 vs Asuncion €395 |
| **CRS Reporting** | YES — full EU tax transparency |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:
- Schengen access to 26 countries
- Mediterranean lifestyle and world-class healthcare
- Large expat community and established infrastructure
- Beckham Law option (first 6 years: only Spanish-source income taxed)

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- EXTREME tax burden: worldwide income up to 47%, plus wealth tax, plus Modelo 720 reporting
- Cannot work AT ALL (even remote work technically restricted)
- 10-year path to citizenship (vs 3 years Paraguay). No dual citizenship for most
- Mandatory 183+ days/year physical presence
- 87% higher cost of living
- CRS reporting to all EU countries
- Wealth tax on global assets over €700K
- NLV does NOT lead to permanent residency as quickly as Paraguay

---

### 2.5 THAILAND — Elite/Privilege Visa & LTR Visa

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | Elite: ฿650K-฿5M one-time fee ($18K-$143K). LTR: $80K/yr income OR $1M assets + $500K Thai investment |
| **Tax on Foreign Income** | Progressive 0-35% on Thai income. Foreign income TAXED IF REMITTED to Thailand (2024 rule change). Prior to 2024, remitted foreign income was exempt. Now: all remitted foreign income is taxable |
| **Time to Residency** | Elite: 30-45 days. LTR: 2-4 months |
| **Physical Presence** | Elite: No min requirement. LTR: No min requirement. But 180+ days triggers tax residency |
| **Path to Citizenship** | Effectively NONE for foreigners. Requires 5+ years residency, Thai language fluency, and is rarely granted. No reliable citizenship pathway |
| **Cost of Living** | ~25-30% more expensive than Paraguay. Bangkok 1BR: ~$500 vs Asuncion $374. But comparable in rural areas |
| **CRS Reporting** | YES — Thailand joined CRS in 2023 |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:
- World-class food, tourism, and hospitality infrastructure
- Large digital nomad/expat community
- Excellent healthcare at low cost
- No physical presence requirement
- Elite visa is simple and fast (pay and get approved)

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- NO path to citizenship (effectively impossible)
- 2024 tax rule change: remitted foreign income now taxable at progressive rates up to 35%
- CRS signatory since 2023
- Elite visa is expensive ($18K-$143K) for what is essentially a renewable long-stay tourist visa
- Must leave annually for visa stamp (Elite) or do 90-day reporting
- Cannot work on Elite visa (separate work permit required)
- Higher cost of living in desirable areas
- Political uncertainty (Thailand has had multiple coups)

---

### 2.6 UAE — Green Visa / Retirement Visa / Freelance Visa

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | Retirement: AED 1M savings ($272K) OR AED 20K/month income ($5.5K/mo). Freelance: AED 360K/yr income ($98K/yr) + bachelor's degree. Green Visa: AED 15K/month salary + bachelor's |
| **Tax on Foreign Income** | 0% personal income tax. NO tax on foreign income. Corporate tax 9% on profits over AED 375K. No wealth tax, no inheritance tax |
| **Time to Residency** | 2-6 weeks for most visa types |
| **Physical Presence** | No minimum for visa maintenance. Must enter every 6 months typically. But 183+ days may trigger home country tax issues |
| **Path to Citizenship** | Effectively NONE. Naturalization only by sovereign decree after 30 years. No reliable pathway |
| **Cost of Living** | ~100-150% higher than Paraguay. Dubai 1BR: $1,500-$2,500/mo vs Asuncion $374. Very expensive |
| **CRS Reporting** | YES — UAE is CRS signatory |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:
- TRUE 0% personal income tax on ALL income (not just foreign)
- World-class infrastructure, airports, and lifestyle
- Dubai is a global business hub
- Fast processing (weeks, not months)
- Excellent banking and financial services

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- NO path to citizenship at all (Paraguay: citizenship in 3 years)
- Extremely high cost of living (2-3x Paraguay)
- No territorial tax nuance — it's simply 0%, but you can't become a citizen
- CRS reporting active
- Visa tied to employment/investment/business — can lose residency if you lose job
- Harsh climate (extreme heat 6+ months/year)
- Culturally less aligned with European expats
- No long-term security — you are always a temporary resident
- Minimum income/investment requirements are significantly higher than Paraguay's standard route

### 2.7 COSTA RICA — Pensionado / Rentista / Inversionista

| Dimension | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Min. Investment/Income** | Pensionado: $1K/mo lifetime pension. Rentista: $2.5K/mo for 2 years OR $60K bank deposit. Inversionista: $150K investment |

**Key Strengths vs Paraguay**:

- Well-established expat communities
- Excellent eco-tourism and outdoor lifestyle

**Key Weaknesses vs Paraguay**:
- Mandatory CAJA (social security) enrollment costs significant % of income
- 3-year temporary residency before permanent (Paraguay: can get permanent directly)
- Citizenship takes 5-7 years vs 3 years in Paraguay
- Higher cost of living across all categories
- CRS reporting active (Paraguay has none)
- Rentista requires $2.5K/mo income for 2 years minimum
- Slower bureaucracy (6-12 months processing)
- Inversionista requires $150K (Paraguay standard route: $0)
- Higher local income tax (25% vs 10%)
- Limited work rights during temporary period

## Summary

### What I Did
Researched the macro competitive landscape for Paraguay residency across all 7 key alternatives: Panama, Uruguay, Portugal D7, Spain NLV, Thailand (Elite/LTR), UAE, and Costa Rica. For each, I gathered current (2026) data on minimum investment/income, tax on foreign income, residency timeline, physical presence requirements, citizenship path, cost of living, and key strengths/weaknesses vs Paraguay.

### What I Found
**Paraguay's competitive position is exceptionally strong** right now due to several factors:

1. **Uruguay's 2026 tax reform DESTROYED its advantage** -- foreign income now taxed at 12% post-holiday, and the tax holiday requires $2M real estate or $100K/yr in innovation fund. Paraguay offers 0% permanently with no investment.

2. **Portugal's NHR regime is dead** -- new D7 holders face progressive income tax up to 48%. No more 10% flat on foreign pensions. Paraguay's 0% is permanent by law.

3. **Spain NLV has the worst tax treatment** of any option: 47% income tax, wealth tax on global assets over €700K, 10-year citizenship path, no work allowed.

4. **Panama requires $300K+ investment** (rising to $500K Oct 2026), while Paraguay's standard route requires $0. Both have territorial tax, but Paraguay has no CRS.

5. **UAE and Thailand offer no citizenship** -- UAE requires 30+ years for naturalization (by decree only), and Thai citizenship is effectively impossible. Paraguay grants citizenship in 3 years.

6. **Costa Rica** has territorial tax but slower citizenship (5-7 yrs), higher CoL, mandatory CAJA, and CRS.

**Paraguay's unique value proposition**: The only jurisdiction offering all of 0% foreign income tax (permanent, no sunset), citizenship in 3 years, zero physical presence requirement for residency, no CRS reporting, and ultra-low cost of living -- with a $0 income threshold for the standard route.
